description: Clay Millennium Prize Problems - statements, partial results, and known approaches.

facts.context: The Riemann Hypothesis concerns the zeros of the Riemann zeta function ζ(s). It states that:
facts.truth: all non-trivial zeros have real part equal to 1/2 (lie on the critical line)
distractors: zeros can have any real part between 0 and 1

facts.context: Computational verification of the Riemann Hypothesis has confirmed:
facts.truth: the first 10+ trillion zeros all lie on the critical line
distractors: only the first 1000 zeros have been checked

facts.context: If the Riemann Hypothesis is true, it would imply:
facts.truth: the prime counting function π(x) has error term O(√x log x)
distractors: no improvement to prime number theorem

facts.context: The P versus NP problem asks whether:
facts.truth: every problem whose solution can be verified in polynomial time can also be solved in polynomial time
distractors: all problems can be solved efficiently
no problems can be solved efficiently
verification is always harder than solving

facts.truth: any proof of P≠NP cannot relativize (cannot work for all oracles)
distractors: there are no barriers to solving P vs NP

facts.context: The natural proofs barrier (Razborov-Rudich) shows that:
facts.truth: if one-way functions exist, certain proof techniques cannot separate P from NP
distractors: natural proofs can solve P vs NP
cryptography is unrelated to P vs NP
all circuit lower bounds are natural proofs

facts.context: The Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness problem asks whether:
facts.truth: smooth solutions exist globally in time for 3D, or if singularities can form

facts.context: For 2D Navier-Stokes, global existence and regularity:
facts.truth: is proven - smooth solutions exist for all time (Ladyzhenskaya 1969)

facts.context: The Hodge Conjecture states that for projective algebraic varieties:
facts.truth: certain cohomology classes (Hodge classes) are rational linear combinations of algebraic cycles

facts.context: The Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer Conjecture relates:
facts.truth: the rank of an elliptic curve to the order of vanishing of its L-function at s=1
distractors: elliptic curves to prime numbers directly
only applies to curves over finite fields
the conjecture has been proven for all curves

facts.context: The BSD conjecture has been proven for elliptic curves:
The Yang-Mills existence and mass gap problem asks to prove:
facts.truth: quantum Yang-Mills theory exists in 4D and has a positive mass gap
